title: malva alcea
intro: Malva alcea, also known as Mallow or Cheeseweed, is a perennial herbaceous plant that belongs to the mallow family (Malvaceae). The roots and leaves of Malva alcea have been used in traditional medicine for their medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and expectorant properties. The plant is rich in mucilages, which have been traditionally used to soothe digestive issues, reduce inflammation, and alleviate respiratory problems, such as bronchitis and asthma. Malva alcea can be prepared as a tea, syrup, or infused oil, and is also used as an ingredient in herbal remedies and salves, making it a valuable addition to natural health practices.
